title: Getting random 403 Forbidden errors on the dashboard
body: I keep getting 403 errors when I try to access the TaskFlow dashboard. It's not every time — sometimes it loads fine, sometimes I get a 403 page. It seems totally random. My permissions haven't changed and other people on my team say they're seeing it too. Started happening a couple days ago.

comments.author: triage-agent
comments.body:
Thanks for reporting this — intermittent 403s are tricky. A couple of things that would help narrow this down:

When you hit the 403, does refreshing the page fix it, or do you have to log out and back in? And have you noticed any pattern at all — for example, does it tend to happen after you've been idle for a while, or right after performing a specific action like saving a task or switching projects?

comments.author: reporter
comments.body: Yeah, refreshing usually fixes it! I just hit refresh and it loads fine the next time, or sometimes it takes a couple refreshes. I don't need to log out or anything. I haven't really noticed a pattern with idle time or specific actions — it seems pretty random. I kind of assumed it was a network issue on our end. I will say it feels like it happens maybe a third of the time? But I'm not sure if that's exact.

comments.author: triage-agent
comments.body:
That's really helpful — the fact that a simple refresh clears it is a strong clue. Two things that would help us zero in on the cause:

1. Do you happen to know if anything changed on your team's setup around when this started? For example, a new deployment, an infrastructure update, or any changes to authentication or permissions configuration?

2. When you hit the 403, does the error page show any extra detail — like a specific error message, a request ID, or does it just say "Forbidden"? If you're able to open your browser's developer tools (Network tab) next time it happens and note the response body or any unusual headers, that would be incredibly useful.

comments.author: reporter
comments.body:
Yeah actually, now that you mention it — I was recently assigned a new role, the 'analyst' role. That was around the same time this started, maybe two days ago? I'm not sure if that's related or just a coincidence. I think there was some kind of deployment or update around then but I don't know the details.

As for the error page, it just says Forbidden — nothing very detailed that I noticed. I'm not super familiar with dev tools but I can try to check next time it happens.

comments.author: triage-agent
comments.body: That timing with the analyst role is really interesting — that could absolutely be related. Quick question: the other people on your team who are also seeing these 403s, do you know if they also have the 'analyst' role? Or are some of them on different roles (like 'editor' or 'admin') and still hitting the same issue? That would help us figure out whether this is tied to that specific role or something broader like the deployment you mentioned.

comments.author: reporter
comments.body: Hmm, good question. I think the others on my team who mentioned it are also analysts — we were all moved to that new role around the same time. I don't think I've heard anyone with admin or editor roles complaining about it, but I'm not 100% sure. I could ask around if that would help.

comments.author: triage-agent
comments.body: That's really useful context — the analyst role timing is a strong lead. One more thing that would help us narrow down the root cause: when you get the 403, is it always on the main dashboard page, or does it also happen when you navigate to other parts of TaskFlow — like viewing a specific project, opening a task, or accessing settings? Basically, is every page a coin-flip, or are there certain pages that seem to trigger it more than others?

comments.author: reporter
comments.body: It happens pretty much everywhere — the dashboard, project pages, individual tasks. It doesn't seem tied to any specific page. It's like any page I try to load has a chance of giving me the 403. When it works, everything works fine though, all the pages load normally.

comments.author: triage-agent
comments.body: [RESOLVED] {"title":"Intermittent 403 Forbidden errors for users with 'analyst' role across all pages","problem":"Users assigned the newly introduced 'analyst' role are experiencing intermittent 403 Forbidden responses on all TaskFlow pages. Approximately one-third of page loads return a 403, and a simple browser refresh typically clears the error. The issue began ~2 days ago, coinciding with both a deployment/update and bulk assignment of the 'analyst' role. No users with admin or editor roles have reported the issue.","root_cause_hypothesis":"The most likely cause is a permission/authorization caching inconsistency related to the new 'analyst' role. Two plausible mechanisms: (1) An authorization cache (server-side or CDN/reverse proxy layer) intermittently serves stale 'deny' responses because the analyst role's permissions were added but the cache was not fully invalidated. (2) A rolling deployment left some backend instances with the updated role-permission mappings and others without, so requests are allowed or denied depending on which instance handles them. The refresh-fixes-it behavior supports both theories — a new request may hit a different cache entry or backend node.","reproduction_steps":["Log in as a user with the 'analyst' role","Navigate to the TaskFlow dashboard or any page","Refresh the page repeatedly (approximately 10-15 times)","Observe that roughly 1 in 3 loads returns a 403 Forbidden","Compare behavior with a user who has the 'admin' or 'editor' role — they should not see 403s"],"environment":"TaskFlow web application, accessed via browser. Issue affects the 'analyst' role specifically. A deployment or infrastructure change occurred approximately 2 days ago. Exact server/infrastructure details unknown from reporter.","severity":"high","impact":"All users with the 'analyst' role are affected. Roughly one-third of page loads fail. Workaround exists (refresh the page), but it degrades productivity and trust. If the analyst role is being rolled out broadly, impact will scale with adoption.","recommended_fix":"1. Check the authorization/permission configuration for the 'analyst' role — verify it has explicit 'allow' entries for all dashboard and page endpoints, not just the absence of 'deny'. 2. Inspect any permission-caching layer (Redis, in-memory cache, CDN rules) for stale entries or inconsistent TTLs related to role lookups. 3. If running multiple backend instances, verify all instances are on the same deployment version with identical role-permission mappings. 4. Review the deployment that coincided with the role rollout for any partial or failed migration of permission data.","proposed_test_case":"Automated test: authenticate as an analyst-role user and make 50 sequential requests to the dashboard endpoint. Assert that all 50 return 200. Run the same test against admin and editor roles as a control. Additionally, add a unit/integration test that verifies the analyst role resolves to the correct permission set on every authorization check (no cache dependency).","information_gaps":["Exact deployment changes made ~2 days ago (deployment logs, changelog)","Whether the application uses a caching layer for authorization checks and its current configuration","Whether multiple backend instances are running and if all are on the same version","Exact number of analysts affected across the organization","Server-side logs or request IDs from failed 403 responses"]}
